memoir_episodes.title: Plague at Sea: The Crew's Pestilential Disease
memoir_episodes.summary: Shortly after entering the Mediterranean, a violent pestilential disease erupted among Digby's crew aboard the Eagle. Within days, sixty of 150 men were stricken. The sick suffered fevers, vomiting, and delirium, with their 'depraved fantasy' causing them to believe the sea was a green meadow. The dead lay unattended in their hammocks. Despite his officers' urging to return home, Digby refused, arguing that England was now the farthest point they could reach, and resolved to press on to Algiers for help.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Outbreak of plague, crew decimation, officers urge return, Digby refuses
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Edward Stradling, Captain Michael
memoir_episodes.places: Mediterranean Sea, Bay of Biscay, Barbary Coast, Algiers

memoir_episodes.title: Departure from England and the Organized Fleet
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby departed from Deal with two ships (the Eagle and the Elizabeth and George) and approximately 250 crewmen. He meticulously organized his fleet, distributing men and provisions, appointing officers, and drafting detailed instructions for communication, rendezvous points, and prize distribution. He studied John Smith's Sea Grammar to master naval terminology and command.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Fleet organization, departure from Deal, detailed sailing instructions drafted
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Edward Stradling, Henry Stradling, Mr Milborne

memoir_episodes.title: The Shadow of the Gunpowder Plot
memoir_episodes.summary: On Bonfire Night 1627, as London celebrates the foiling of the Gunpowder Plot, Digby paces his chambers haunted by the memory of his father Everard's execution. The annual celebrations force him to confront the 'foul stain in his blood' and strengthen his resolve to depart on his voyage to redeem the family name.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Bonfire Night, memory of father's execution, resolve to voyage

memoir_episodes.title: Falling in Love with Venetia Stanley
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby meets Venetia Stanley through their Catholic families in Buckinghamshire. He is captivated by her beauty, intelligence, and character. Their first kiss occurs secretly during a hunting expedition near Gayhurst, sheltered under a tree during a rainstorm. His mother opposes the match because Venetia is poor.
memoir_episodes.key_events: First meeting, falling in love, first kiss during hunt, mother's opposition
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Venetia Stanley, Mary Digby

memoir_episodes.title: Initiation into Alchemy with Richard Napier
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby studies with Richard Napier, a physician-priest at Great Linford who introduces him to astrology, alchemy, and natural philosophy. Napier casts his horoscope and teaches him to read the stars. Kenelm borrows 'whole cloak-bags' of books from Napier's library, developing his lifelong passion for learning.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Study with Napier, horoscope cast, introduction to alchemy, library borrowing

memoir_episodes.title: The Grand Tour: France, Italy, Spain
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby travels through Europe, encountering Marie de Medici in France, immersing himself in Italian culture at Florence and Siena, and joining Prince Charles and Buckingham in Madrid during the Spanish Match. These travels transform him into a cosmopolitan figure with connections across Catholic Europe.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Encounter with Marie de Medici, Italian sojourn, Spanish Match
memoir_episodes.people: Marie de Medici, Prince Charles, Duke of Buckingham

memoir_episodes.title: Preparing the Voyage Against Buckingham's Opposition
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby assembles a fleet and crew in London while facing deliberate obstruction from the Duke of Buckingham. He negotiates ship purchases, recruits sailors scarred from the disasters at Cadiz and the Ile de Re, and stockpiles provisions. His determination to overcome Buckingham's interference reveals both his ambition and the political dangers surrounding him.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Fleet assembly, Buckingham's obstruction, crew recruitment
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Duke of Buckingham, King Charles I

memoir_episodes.title: Crossing the Bay of Biscay and Entering the Mediterranean
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby's ships cross the Bay of Biscay, round Cape St Vincent, and sneak through the Straits of Gibraltar at night to avoid Spanish ships. He smells rosemary wafting from the Spanish coast, triggering memories of his earlier travels and philosophical reflections on action at a distance.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Bay of Biscay crossing, rounding Cape St Vincent, sneaking through Gibraltar, rosemary scent
memoir_episodes.places: Bay of Biscay, Cape St Vincent, Straits of Gibraltar

memoir_episodes.title: Algiers: Healing, Diplomacy, and Freeing Slaves
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby brings his plague-stricken fleet into Algiers, negotiates with the local authorities, treats his sick crew, and arranges the release of English captives held as slaves. The stay combines practical crisis management with intellectual exploration in one of the Mediterranean's most cosmopolitan cities.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Arrival at Algiers, crew recovery, negotiation with authorities, freeing slaves

memoir_episodes.title: The Battle of Scanderoon
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby's fleet engages Venetian galleasses in the Bay of Scanderoon, winning a decisive naval victory. The aftermath brings complications: English merchants are imprisoned in Aleppo, Venetians spread damaging rumors calling Digby a pirate, and the vice consul pleads repeatedly for him to leave.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Naval battle, victory over Venetians, English merchants imprisoned, pirate label
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Antonio Capello, Edward Stradling, Thomas Roe

memoir_episodes.title: Becalmed off Egypt: Reading Heliodorus
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby's ships are becalmed off the Egyptian coast for five sweltering days. He retreats to his cabin and reads Heliodorus's Aethiopica, a Greek romance about star-crossed lovers wandering the Mediterranean. The tale deeply resonates with his own experiences and inspires him to write his own memoir in the same style.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Becalmed off Egypt, reading Heliodorus, philosophical reflection, sweet Arabian winds

memoir_episodes.title: Treasure Hunting at Delos
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby spends three days on the deserted island of Delos collecting ancient marbles from the ruins of Apollo's temple. He devises an ingenious pulley system to hoist a massive marble block and stands for an hour contemplating a colossal broken statue of Apollo, too large to move.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Marble collection, pulley engineering, contemplation of Apollo statue, inscribed monument

memoir_episodes.title: Reading Spenser at Sea: Alchemy in Poetry
memoir_episodes.summary: While sailing around Greece, Digby reads Spenser's Faerie Queene and becomes fascinated by a dense stanza describing a castle representing the human body. He interprets it through the lenses of alchemy and astrology, finding references to salt, sulphur, mercury, the seven planets, and the nine angelic orders.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Reading Spenser, alchemical interpretation, letter to Stradling

memoir_episodes.title: The Homeward Voyage
memoir_episodes.summary: Digby drives westward through the Mediterranean, capturing additional prizes, dodging Spanish fleets, and visiting Lampedusa. Near Sardinia he captures a Hamburg merchant ship carrying Granadan wool. He passes through the Straits of Gibraltar and crosses the Atlantic, arriving home after thirteen months at sea.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Prize captures, news of Buckingham confirmed, passing Lampedusa, homeward crossing
memoir_episodes.places: Sicily, Malta, Lampedusa, Sardinia, Straits of Gibraltar, London

memoir_episodes.title: The Maria de' Medici Affair in Angers
memoir_episodes.summary: During his Grand Tour, plague forced Digby from Paris to Angers, where the exiled Queen Regent Maria de' Medici attempted to seduce him. He resisted out of fidelity to Venetia. During a military assault on the city, he faked his own death and fled France. His letters home were intercepted by his mother, leading Venetia to believe him dead.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Maria de Medici's seduction attempt, faked death, flight from Angers, intercepted letters
memoir_episodes.people: Kenelm Digby, Maria de Medici, Venetia Stanley, Mary Digby

memoir_episodes.title: Childhood Love: Theagenes and Stelliana
memoir_episodes.summary: Stelliana (Venetia), orphaned of her mother as an infant, is raised near the home of Arete (Lady Digby). The children Theagenes and Stelliana develop a precocious mutual attachment, but are separated when Stelliana's father reclaims her.
memoir_episodes.key_events: First meetings as children, early attachment, separation by Nearchus/Sir Edward Stanley
memoir_episodes.people: Theagenes/Kenelm, Stelliana/Venetia, Nearchus/Sir Edward Stanley, Arete/Lady Digby

memoir_episodes.title: The Abduction by Ursatius
memoir_episodes.summary: Faustina, bribed by the powerful nobleman Ursatius, lures Stelliana into a coach that carries her to his country lodge. He professes love; she resists. She escapes by lowering herself from a window using sheets, is attacked by a wolf in the woods, and rescued by the young nobleman Mardontius.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Faustina's betrayal, abduction, captivity, escape through window, wolf attack, rescue by Mardontius

memoir_episodes.title: Secret Meeting During the Stag Hunt
memoir_episodes.summary: At Artesia's house, the lovers manage a secret meeting during a hunt. Theagenes slips a note into Stelliana's glove. They exchange tokens — a diamond ring and a lock of hair — and vow constancy before his departure abroad.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Note in glove, secret rendezvous during hunt, exchange of ring and hair, vows of constancy

memoir_episodes.title: The Brahmin and the Spirit of Stelliana
memoir_episodes.summary: Traveling to Spain, Theagenes encounters a Brahmin who engages him in philosophical dialogue about astrology and free will. The Brahmin conjures a spirit in Stelliana's form, which reveals her honor is intact and prophesies that Theagenes will kill two men in a fight and that the lovers are fated to unite.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Meeting the Brahmin, philosophical debate, spirit conjuration, prophecy of combat, revelation of Stelliana's innocence
memoir_episodes.people: Theagenes/Kenelm, the Brahmin, spirit of Stelliana

memoir_episodes.title: The Night Ambush in Madrid
memoir_episodes.summary: In Madrid, Theagenes and Leodivius are ambushed by fifteen armed men lying in wait over a jealous quarrel. Theagenes fights them single-handedly, killing two including the leader, fulfilling the spirit's prophecy. Prince Charles and Buckingham arrive incognito the next day.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Night ambush, single combat against fifteen, killing of two men, fulfillment of prophecy, arrival of Prince Charles
memoir_episodes.people: Theagenes/Kenelm, Leodivius, Aristobulus/Earl of Bristol, Prince Charles, Hephaestion/Buckingham

memoir_episodes.title: Stelliana Pawns Her Jewels
memoir_episodes.summary: When Theagenes faces financial difficulty for an embassy appointment, Stelliana pawns her jewels and plate to fund his expenses. This act of selfless generosity finally tips the balance: Theagenes resolves to marry her, overcoming all his remaining doubts.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Financial crisis, Stelliana pawns jewels, Theagenes resolves to marry, Clericius's rejected suit

memoir_episodes.title: The Challenge to Mardontius and the Portrait
memoir_episodes.summary: Stelliana refuses to marry while Mardontius possesses her portrait, given under a promise. Theagenes challenges Mardontius to a duel; Mardontius declines and surrenders the portrait with a written declaration of Stelliana's honor. The obstacle removed, they marry secretly.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Portrait as obstacle, duel challenge, Mardontius surrenders, secret marriage ceremony

memoir_episodes.title: The Postscript at Milos: Writing the Memoir
memoir_episodes.summary: Stranded on Milos after a storm separated his fleet, Digby explains he wrote the memoir to occupy himself while refusing the company of local women offered by his host. He pretended to write dispatches but instead set down his 'loose fantasies' as a private record, requesting that the manuscript be burned if found.
memoir_episodes.key_events: Storm separates fleet, stranded on Milos, temptation refused, composition of memoir, plea for destruction
